close_reason: Unavoidable: spawn_blocking requires Send + 'static, forcing a clone of the PIN. The AuthPin wrapper provides zeroization on drop for each copy. This is inherent to async + FFI boundaries.
description:
Trigger: PIN material lives in the process for the lifetime of the Pkcs11Backend.

What's there now: user_pin: Option<Zeroizing<String>> in the struct. Zeroizing<String> calls .zeroize() on Drop, which zeroes the heap-allocated bytes. Good baseline.

Concerns:
1. Every async method clones user_pin (lib.rs:322, 385, 469, 531) into a fresh Zeroizing<String>. Each clone allocates a new heap buffer. Drop of each clone zeroes — fine — but during the spawn_blocking task lifetime there are extra plaintext copies on the heap.
2. AuthPin::new(pin.as_str().into()) at lib.rs:212, 236 creates yet another copy whose zeroization depends on cryptoki::types::AuthPin's impl. Verify cryptoki guarantees zeroization on Drop. If not, every login leaves a PIN copy in memory until the allocator reuses it.
3. String can grow at internal points; on grow, the old buffer is freed without zeroization. For an immutable PIN String this isn't triggered, but if any code path were to push to it, the residue would persist.

Action: (a) audit cryptoki::types::AuthPin to confirm it zeroizes on Drop. If yes, document the guarantee chain in a SECURITY.md note. If not, file an upstream issue and consider boxing the PIN into a SecretString from secrecy crate. (b) document the per-call clone cost as a known limitation — it's the price of sending PIN to spawn_blocking. (c) consider switching to Zeroizing<Box<[u8]>> for the PIN so capacity == length and grow can't drop a non-zeroized buffer.
title: PIN copies in spawn_blocking closures and AuthPin reduce zeroization guarantees
